data.description:
## Why

Once planned-trace parity holds (B4), the gates can be switched from the bespoke orchestrator /
direct-agent path to the engine workflow path — converging on one substrate — WITHOUT regressing
the claim/close gate behaviour or the signed verdicts/attestations CI depends on.

## What

Cut the claim gate (plan-review) and the close gate (completion-verification) over to the engine
workflow path behind a config flag, defaulting to the workflow path once parity is proven. The
signing/attestation surfaces (`attest.py` plan-review signatures; the `completion_verdict` HMAC
signing) must keep producing byte-compatible signed artifacts so existing closed/claimed tickets
verify and CI's signature checks still pass. The bespoke path stays reachable behind the flag as a
fallback until removed.

## Scope

- Wire `_commands/claim.py` (plan-review precheck) and `_commands/transition.py` (close verify) +
  the library entrypoints (`review_plan` / `verify_completion`) to dispatch through the workflow
  engine, behind a config flag.
- Preserve the signed-verdict / attestation formats (material fingerprint binding, manifest
  prefixes) so verification is unchanged.
- Integration tests: claim gate + close gate still pass/fail correctly via the workflow path; signed
  artifacts verify.
- Depends on B4 (parity must hold first).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The claim (plan-review) and close (completion-verification) gates dispatch through the engine
      workflow path behind a config flag, defaulting to the workflow path.
- [ ] Signed verdicts/attestations remain byte-compatible (material binding + manifest prefixes
      unchanged); previously signed tickets still verify and CI signature checks pass.
- [ ] Integration tests cover claim+close pass/fail through the workflow path with intact signing;
      the bespoke path remains reachable behind the flag as a fallback.
- [ ] `make lint` + the full test suite pass.
data.title: B5: Cut the claim/close gates over to the workflow path (signing intact)
